mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without requiring a specific trigger threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth and retail sales jointly signal aggregate demand strength. When both GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth are simultaneously positive over a quarter, equity valuations expand due to earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism requires joint confirmation from both demand proxies.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both show positive growth over prior quarter
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns when both proxies are positive do not exceed baseline by at least 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1% year-over-year signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as it indicates sustainable earnings growth without inflationary pressure. The mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of positive INDPRO growth do not exceed negative growth periods by at least 1.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100k new jobs per month, signaling labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and inflation risk. This triggers a positive equity response over 20 trading days as the Fed is less likely to maintain restrictive policy. The mechanism activates only when employment growth falls below the specified threshold.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 100k jobs
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure consumer and construction demand. When both decline simultaneously by more than 5% month-over-month, it signals demand destruction that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth downward. The mechanism requires joint confirmation from both demand indicators.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns when both proxies decline are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) remains above 3% year-over-year, the Fed is in a restrictive policy regime that suppresses equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the terminal rate is likely to remain elevated.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during restrictive regimes are positive in > 55% of instances (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines by more than 1% month-over-month, signaling a supply shock or demand destruction that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism activates only when the production decline exceeds the specified threshold, indicating a material deterioration in economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 1-year rolling window.
